title: CLI envelope
description: Universal envelope wrapping every hpc-mapreduce stdout response. The `$defs` block at the bottom holds reusable subschemas — run_id (loose + strict), combined_waves, failed_waves, lifecycle_state (terminal / observable / observable+timeout), scheduler, gpu_type, error_code. Note: at the time of writing, the agent_cli's jsonschema.validate path does not register a RefResolver, so cross-file `$ref` does not resolve; consumer schemas must therefore inline these definitions verbatim. `$defs` here is the canonical source of truth, and tests/test_schema_defs_consistency.py asserts every inline copy stays byte-equivalent.

run_id.description: Run identifier — typically YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS-<short_sha>; loosely validated as filesystem-safe with no path separators.

run_id_strict.description: Strict run-identifier shape used on input — must be filesystem-safe (alphanumerics, dot, underscore, hyphen). Output schemas use the looser `run_id` ({type: string}) so legacy sidecars validate.

combined_waves.description: Wave numbers whose partial-aggregate file has been combined into the run's _combiner/ directory.

failed_waves.description: Wave numbers whose combine attempt exhausted retries; the run may still be partially aggregated.

lifecycle_state_terminal.description: Lifecycle state set used by monitor-flow output. 'complete' = every task reported complete. 'failed' = at least one failure with no running/pending tasks left. 'abandoned' = recorded job_ids are no longer known to the scheduler. 'timeout' = wall_clock_budget_seconds exceeded; cluster jobs may still be running.

lifecycle_state_observable.description: Lifecycle state set used by status / reconcile (point-in-time observation, no 'timeout' bucket).

lifecycle_state_observable_with_timeout.description: Lifecycle state set used when an observer also surfaces 'timeout' (e.g. status / reconcile reading sidecars previously marked timeout by monitor-flow). Superset of lifecycle_state_observable.

scheduler.description: Scheduler driver. 'sge' covers Sun/Univa/Open Grid Engine variants; 'slurm' covers Slurm-Workload-Manager clusters.

gpu_type.description: Cluster-specific GPU label (e.g. 'A100', 'H100', 'L40S'). Validated as a non-empty string; semantic checks (does this cluster expose this GPU?) live in inspect-cluster, not the schema.

error_code.description: Canonical envelope error_code enum. Output schemas that surface error codes inside `data` (e.g. failures, status, validate) must keep their enum byte-equivalent to this list.

partial_errors.description: Optional top-level array surfacing soft per-source failures while the operation as a whole succeeded. Distinct from `data.errors`: this key documents *cluster-side* degradations (qhost timed out, one of N nodes unreachable, sacct unavailable) that the planner considered and chose to tolerate. Operations that genuinely failed should set ok:false instead.
